parityMeta.describes: PA-FIX (S7 hash fidelity) hashProgramTemplate case-folding tie-break parity — a synthetic template whose open-bag object carries keys that are EQUAL once lowercased (AB/ab/Ab/aB and zz/ZZ). The inline stableStringify sorts keys by JS String.prototype.localeCompare, whose tertiary case tie-break sorts lower-BEFORE-upper (ab,aB,Ab,AB,zz,ZZ) — the EXACT INVERSE of the §9 canonicalKeyOrder raw code-point tie-break (upper-before-lower). Existing hash-cases-v1 never carries two keys equal-when-lowercased, so this branch was unexercised and the keyOrderLess case tie-break could byte-drift undetected; this fixture pins it. Generated from src/engines/programAdjustmentEngine.ts; the Swift port (with the keyOrderLess tie-break corrected to localeCompare-faithful) EXACT-asserts the hash string == golden.
parityMeta.sourceNotes: No clock — pure stableStringify + FNV-1a transform. Synthetic template; no email/token/userId. The caseFoldProbe keys exercise the localeCompare case tertiary tie-break only.

cases.label: hashProgramTemplate: case-folding tie-break — keys equal when lowercased sort lower-before-upper (localeCompare tertiary, NOT §9 code-point)
